title: aify-project-graph tasks overlay
description: Task snapshot bound to features. Lives at `.aify-graph/tasks.json`. Written by /graph-build-tasks from an external tracker (ClickUp/Asana/Linear/Jira/GitHub) or hand-authored for local work. Permissively loaded; see docs/schema-versions.md.

version.description: Schema version of THIS overlay (not the graph DB). Currently '0.1'.

source.description: Where tasks came from (e.g. 'clickup:space-123', 'asana:project-456', 'local'). Stored for provenance + re-sync reference.

fetched_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of the last sync from the tracker.

id.description: External tracker ID (e.g. 'CU-8234', 'ENG-412', '#123') OR local ID (e.g. 'T-local-42'). /graph-task-edit warns when editing tracker IDs locally — those don't sync back.

title.description: Short task title. Shown in brief.plan.md task-preview lines.

status.description: Tracker-defined status (e.g. 'open', 'in-progress', 'done', 'blocked'). Open-vs-closed is derived by brief generator — see taskFeatureRefs() in generator.js.

features.description: Feature IDs this task belongs to. Preferred binding. Loader accepts `related_features` as alias for compatibility with older writers.

related_features.description: Legacy alias for `features`. Accepted for read-compatibility; new writers should use `features`.

labels.description: Free-form labels from the tracker (e.g. 'bug', 'P1', 'regression'). /graph-walk-bugs can filter on these.

priority.description: Tracker-defined priority string. Free-form so we don't lock into one tracker's vocabulary.

url.description: Link back to the tracker item.

assignee.description: Who owns the task (tracker user identifier).

evidence.description: Why this task was bound to these features. Written by /graph-build-tasks so attribution is auditable. Prefer machine-readable prefixes such as `tag:`, `commit:`, `branch:`, `path:`, `title:`, or `spec:` so brief generation can distinguish strong code-anchored links from broader/spec mappings.

link_strength.description: Optional explicit attribution strength. `strong` = direct code/tracker binding (tag/path/commit/branch), `mixed` = several weaker but consistent signals, `broad` = future/spec/title-only mapping. If omitted, the brief generator infers a best-effort tier from `evidence` and `files_hint`.
